summary.intent: Fix a Rust formatting issue in the skills crate and commit/push the change.
summary.outcome: Formatting was fixed in crates/skills/src/parse.rs and committed. The push was initiated but the transcript does not confirm it completed.

repo: The project uses a `just` recipe called `format-check` (line 14) for Rust formatting enforcement, run as part of parallel local checks alongside biome, i18n, and zizmor.
CI includes zizmor for GitHub Actions security auditing across 7 workflow/action files with 28 suppressed findings.
i18n parity is validated automatically: 3 locales, 18 namespaces, 133 files checked.

code.finding: Nightly rustfmt reformatted a chained `.or_else()` closure from a multi-line assignment into a more compact style.
Nightly rustfmt also reformatted a multi-argument format string call to place each argument on its own line.

workflow: The project uses nightly rustfmt, which can produce formatting diffs that differ from stable rustfmt — format-check failures may appear even when code looks reasonably formatted.
Local checks run in parallel; a single failure (fmt) fails the whole suite even if all other checks pass.

friction: Formatting check failed after what appeared to be already-passing code, requiring a separate fix-and-recommit cycle.
Nightly rustfmt style differences are a silent gotcha — the diff was purely cosmetic but blocked the commit.
